articles.title: Peace Talks Head to Pakistan: Can the War with Iran End?
articles.summary: A U.S. team is flying to Pakistan to talk with Iran about ending the war. The talks focus on the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway where ships are stuck. Iran's foreign minister is already there, but says no meeting is planned with Americans. The White House says Iran wants to talk. The war has been going on for two months. A ceasefire has held for three weeks. The U.S. has three aircraft carriers in the region. Oil prices are high because of the war. The U.S. also announced new sanctions on Iran and Chinese companies.

articles.title: Court Says No: Trump's Asylum Ban Is Illegal
articles.summary: A court ruled that President Trump's order to stop people from asking for asylum at the southern border is illegal. Asylum is when someone fleeing danger asks to stay in another country. The court said immigration laws give people the right to apply for asylum. The White House said it was "unsurprising" and blamed judges. The government can appeal to the Supreme Court. Advocates say denying asylum puts people in danger. The ruling doesn't change much right now because a previous court had already paused the ban.

articles.title: Justice Department Drops Investigation of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
articles.summary: The U.S. Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. The probe was about cost overruns in renovating two Fed buildings. A judge had blocked the investigation, calling it an improper attempt to pressure the Fed. The U.S. attorney said she will ask the Fed's inspector general to look into the costs instead. This clears the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh as the next Fed chair. Powell's term ends next month. The Fed is important because it sets interest rates that affect the economy.
